title: InstCombine: Stop transforming EQ/NE of SHR to 0 to ULT/UGT if >1 use
summary: The developers generalized an optimization by recognizing that the original pattern—transforming `icmp eq/ne (shr X, ShAmt), 0` into a simpler unsigned comparison—was too narrow. The original code only handled the `C == 0` case for `lshr`/`ashr` with equality predicates. They removed this special-case logic, allowing the subsequent general transformation (canonicalizing the shift into an `and` mask) to apply more broadly. This canonicalization works for any constant `C`, not just zero, and for both `eq` and `ne` predicates, as long as the shift has one use. The removal also implicitly extends support to other shift types and predicates by letting later folds handle them. The key insight: a specific zero-comparison fold was redundant and restrictive; eliminating it enabled the existing general `and`-mask pattern to cover all cases, simplifying code and increasing optimization coverage.
